programmes.show_description: Dreaming of Thee. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie's 3 weeks from release from prison with time off for good behaviour. However, Charles Endell visits him on visiting day.
1970. Comedy. Director: Brian De Palma. Stars Robert De Niro & Allen Garfield. A Vietnam veteran returns to New York and begins filmmaking, but he soon falls in with a group of radical activists.
A rare treat, courtesy of a Dutch airline, about air travel in the 1960s.
To Wear a Badge. 1961. Stars Stephen McNally, Robert Harland & Harold J. Stone. Investigative reporters Paul Marino & Jack Flood investigate various cases of industrial corruption. (S1, E10)
Good Deed. Stars Keefe Brasselle, Jay C. Flippen & James Best. British actor David Niven, introduces this 1950s US anthology series. (S1, E12)
Granny Versus the Weather Bureau. 1964. Stars Irene Ryan, John McGiver & Quinn O'Hara. Granny's old-fashioned ways of predicting the weather are pitted against the Weather Bureau's technology.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life One for the Road
programmes.show_description: 1965. A thought provoking look at the problem of drinking and driving and how it is being tackled.
1942. Mystery. Director: Edward Dmytryk. Stars Chester Morris, Harriet Hilliard & Richard Lane. A reformed thief becomes involved in a case to find a woman's missing sculpture of Augustus Caesar.
1948. Crime Drama starring John Bentley & Dinah Sheridan. Scotland Yard is unable to unravel the 'Rex' murders and cannot manage without the help of Paul Temple and his sidekick 'Steve'.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Where Have All...
programmes.show_description: ... the Butterflies Gone? 1968. An enchanting look into the natural world of the butterfly and the reasons for the apparent shortage of them.
1959. Drama. Director: Godfrey Grayson. Stars Sean Lynch, Beth Rogan & Raymond Huntley. A teenager is falsely accused of robbery. A young girl must persuade him to give himself up.
1949. Comedy. Directed by & stars Robert Donat. Features Reneé Asherson & Thord Hird. A sergeant returns from the war and tries to reconcile with his fiancée but winds up falling for a new lodger.
1961. Crime Drama. Directed by Michael Winner. Stars Terence Longden, Donald Gray and Diane Clare. A reporter learns that his brother, a student at Cambridge, has killed himself or so it seems.
Blackbeard. 1956. Stars Alec Clunes, George Margo & Peter Hammond. Blackbeard becomes suspicious when a new governor arrives in the Bahamas and offers the pirates pardons from the King.
The Knock on the door. 1976. Stars Jean Anderson, Patrick O'Connell & Richard Easton. Brian rejects Jane's budget, Barbara questions Brian on his divorce and Gwen gets a visit from the police.
Illegal Entry. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Walter Gotell & Adrienne Corri. Maguire gains insight into a recent wave of robberies after a man is arrested for skulking around on the roof of a shop.
1964. Thriller. Director: Bryan Forbes. Stars Richard Attenborough, Nanette Newman & Kim Stanley. A medium stages a kidnapping which she will then 'solve' by pretending to be psychic.
A short travelogue describing the history of Kent in 1954, with a look at the towns that made up the Cinque Ports.
1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. Narrated by Richard Todd, looking at the life of James Justice Robertson.
Pannage/Dry Fly Fishing on the River Test. 1981. Jack Hargreaves watches woodland pigs foraging for acorns at pannage time. He then continues his album of dry fly fishing on the River Test.
1957. The White Cliffs Mystery. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Starring Russell Napier, Yvonne Andre and Peter Bathurst. A dead rocket engineer was being blackmailed. But why - and by whom? (S1, E25)
A Kind of Cuckoo. 1980. Stars Bryan Marshall, Shirley Anne Field & Clifford Rose. Monica digs into Burton's past and the new board clashes over control, cash and contracts.
1949. Drama. Directed by Sidney Cole, Charles Crichton and Basil Dearden. Starring Jack Warner, Susan Shaw and Gladys Henson. A train disaster is told as four short stories.
1988. Crime Drama. Directed by Colin Luke. Stars Amanda Donohoe, Stephen Dillane & Hilary Mason. A young novelist falls for a married woman, who then wants him to kill her husband.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Men Against the Sea
programmes.show_description: 1965. For centuries the Dutch have fought against the sea. This film takes a look at the Delta Plan - a spectacular sea-defence scheme in Holland, and what it aims to achieve.
And the Lord Taketh Away. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie is out of prison and living with his wife Jean in Watford. But soon he is back in his old Soho hunting ground.

programmes.show_name: Sherlock Holmes: The Sign of Four
programmes.show_description: 1987. Thriller. Director: Peter Hammond. Stars Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke, Jenny Seagrove. A young lady contacts Sherlock Holmes for his help regarding her father who disappeared 10 years ago.
1932. Drama directed by Maurice Elvey and starring Mary Newcomb and Owen Nares. The story of Lillian who has an illegitimate war baby adopted by a wealthy spinster. Filmed at Twickenham Studios.
The Case of the Cunningham Heritage. 1954. Starring: Ronald Howard, H. Marion Crawford & Archie Duncan. Sherlock and Watson investigate a murder, with a young lady as the prime suspect.
1959. The Freight Line. Western with Peter Breck. After his brothers are killed in a shootout, a gunfighter decides to hang up his guns and uphold the law by becoming a lawyer.
1952. Crime Drama. Directed by Charles Saunders. Stars Patrick Barr, Jane Baxter & Raymond Young. When a village doctor's wife is unexpectedly murdered, the new physician sets out to find answers.
1942. Comedy. Director: Harold French & Paul L. Stein. Stars Hugh Williams & Carla Lehmann. A woman meets a researcher. When they marry, he runs across some scandalous gossip about her past.
Directed by Francis Searle. With Fidelma Murphy, Kevin McHugh, Tony Rohr, Patrick Duggan. It's Saturday night in Dublin, and Mick is attempting to chat up Cecilia.
1971. Drama. Director: James Hill. Stars Mark Lester, Walter Slezak & Peter Lee Lawrence. The story of a horse who is separated from a young boy and his life with different owners over the years.
1963. A Scopitone promo for this Joe Meek combo, English rock group The Tornados.
Knight with the Red Plume. 1956. Stars: William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. Sir Lancelot is challenged before he can become a member of King Arthur's Knights of the Round Table.
1982. Drama. Director: Clive Donner. Stars George C. Scott, Tim Curry & Michael Hordern. The classic tale of an orphan boy who finds himself joining a band of thieves after escaping the orphanage.
Special Edition. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Richard Bennet & Donald Bisset. A gang of career criminals believe they have the perfect alibi when they carry out a payroll robbery in Fleet Street.
A Case of Murder - Part 1. 1974. Stars John Barron & Dorothy Vernon. The Margaret and Kenneth Povey have been accused of the murder of Charles Oldbury and are on trial.
A Case of Murder - Part 2. 1974. Stars David Savile & Kathleen Byron. The Judge tells the Jury to disregard the defence's blame on Dr. Fulmer. It is found that Mr Oldbury didn't like the Povey's.
A Case of Murder - Part 3. 1974. Stars Christopher Hancock & Kenneth Watson. Margaret reveals something shocking about Leonard Saxton, the main benefactor of Mr Oldbury's Will.
2017. Director: Mark Kidel. Cary Grant, one of Hollywood's greatest stars, was troubled most of his life with self doubt and insecurity. In his fifties he began taking LSD to exorcise his demons.
1956. The Case of the River Morgue. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Starring Hugh Moxey, Gordon Needham and Jane Welsh. The police are summoned when a dead body is stolen. (S1, E16)
Heaven and Back/Borley Rectory. 1994. Michael Aspel introduces tales of the paranormal, including a man who survived after tumbling 60ft down a cliff and the legend of Borley Rectory, in Essex.
Wheels and Deals. 1973. Stars Jean Anderson, Jennifer Wilson & Gabrielle Drake. Mary learns an ex-girlfriend of Edward's is back on the scene. The Board consider a take-over bid for Carter's firm.
Diamond Jubilee. 1958. Donald Gray, Diana Decker & Robert Ayres. Mac spent ten years in jail for stealing and hiding diamonds. The police have not found them, so Mark Saber is hired to follow Mac.

programmes.show_name: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club
programmes.show_description: Episode 28. 1975. In this jam packed episode, there are brilliant performances from The Chants, Joe 'Mr Piano' Henderson, George Roper, Lorne Lesley, Jim Bowen and The Hermits.
1956. War. Directed by Julian Amyes. Stars George Baker, Stanley Baker & Michael Caine in his first credited film role. In the Korean war, British soldiers try to fight their way back to safety.
Project Sahara. 1970. Stars Robert Powell, Nigel Stock & Hildegard Neil. Doomwatch comes under suspicion. A mysterious department of security services uses a new computer to vet the scientists.
1961. Mystery. Directed by Bernard Wiesen. Stars Mala Powers, Jacques Bergerac & John Harding. A woman is accused of murder after finding a dead woman on a train, but can she uncover the truth?
